coupling.note: Fixes A-D (app-side i18n) are well-isolated leaf modules. Fix E introduces new coupling between desktop IPC layer and i18n by bridging renderer locale state to main process — creates a dependency where crash recovery now depends on IPC locale sync that hasn't survived a crash. Fixes F-G are local to windows.ts with no new deps.

debuggability.note: Fix C has no Intl.PluralRules availability guard — if unavailable on an older runtime, developer sees ReferenceError with no log context. Fix E IPC failure on renderer crash silently shows English with no console.warn. Fix F lacks try-catch on titleBarOverlay for Linux/Wayland — unsupported compositors crash silently. No structured logging or span annotations on any of these paths.

convergence.note: All 7 fixes converge toward a known correct state: locale parity with en.ts, CLDR pluralization, Intl formatting, i18n-native dialogs, consistent titlebar across Win/Linux, macOS vibrancy. Fix E is the weakest — the mechanism (IPC-based locale during crash) doesn't converge toward the standard pattern used by production Electron apps (bootstrap locale from cmdline/env + electron-store + proactive sync).

surface_area.note: 18 locale files + 1 test + 2 new modules (plural.ts, format.ts) + 1 new module (dialog-strings.ts) + windows.ts + ipc.ts + preload types + preload index + constants = ~25-28 files changed. The surface area is proportionate to the problem but Fix E alone touches 6 files (dialog-strings.ts, windows.ts, ipc.ts, constants.ts, preload/types.ts, preload/index.ts) for what could be a simpler approach.

testability.note: Fixes A-D are trivially testable in Bun (parity.test.ts for A-B, bun -e for C-D). Fix E requires manual smoke testing across platforms (Electron native dialog). Fix F-G require manual visual verification on Linux and macOS. The parity test removes the existing check that locale translations differ from English — the test will pass even if all keys are English placeholders.

error_clarity.note: Fix C: Intl.PluralRules throws ReferenceError with no useful context if missing. Fix E: IPC locale fallback to English is silent — developer sees English dialog buttons in a non-English locale with no log entry. Fix F: titleBarOverlay on unsupported Linux compositors either throws or silently fails depending on Electron version — no user-visible signal. Fix G: vibrancy unsupported similarly silent.

reversibility.note: 7 independent fixes in one plan with shared files (windows.ts, language.tsx, ipc.ts, preload/types.ts, preload/index.ts) makes clean rollback impossible. Reverting Fix F/G requires untangling from other changes to windows.ts. Reverting Fix E means reverting IPC handler additions that other future code might depend on. Each fix should be its own commit, ideally its own plan.

objections.detail: Fix E: `handle()` checks `button === "Export Logs"` and `button === "Relaunch"` — if buttons are localized, these string comparisons always fail. The clicked button index (`result.response`) is available but unused for routing.
Fix C: `tPlural` references free variable `dict` that isn't imported or passed. The code `t(localeKey in dict ? ...)` will produce a ReferenceError at runtime.

Fix E: Recovery dialog fires on render-process-gone (windows.ts:335) — at that point, the renderer is dead and IPC to get locale returns null. No proactive sync mechanism described. Dialog always shows English for crash scenarios.

Fix E: DIALOG_STRINGS contains English-only strings. There's no path shown from the app's i18n locale files (packages/app/src/i18n/) to the desktop main process (packages/desktop/src/main/). The strings will never be translated unless a cross-package locale bridge is built.

Fix F: win.setTitleBarOverlay() may throw on Linux/Wayland. Plan mentions 'guard with try-catch' but code sketch doesn't implement it. Without guard, users on unsupported compositors get a runtime error.

Fix B: The proposed test only checks key existence, not actual translation. The existing test also checks `expect(locale[key]).not.toBe(en[key])`. Combined with Fix A filling gaps with English text, the test will pass with all-English locales.

Plan conflates 7 independent fixes across app and desktop packages. Fixes A-D (app i18n), E (desktop IPC/dialogs), and F-G (platform UI) share no code but are bundled together, making independent validation and rollback harder.

conditions_for_approval: Fix button label comparison in handle() — use result.response index instead of English button label string
Add `dict` parameter or import to tPlural — the free variable reference won't compile
Add proactive locale sync from renderer to main process (IPC handler set-locale-preference + electron-store) so crash recovery can read stored locale
Either (a) add cross-package locale bridge so dialog strings are actually localized, or (b) explicitly scope dialog i18n to English-only and file follow-up
Add try-catch around win.setTitleBarOverlay() in updateTitlebar for Linux/Wayland support
Retain or document the removal of the 'not equal to English' assertion in parity.test.ts — without it, the test doesn't distinguish between 'parity' and 'all English placeholders'
follow_ups: Split plan into 3 independent lanes: app-i18n-core (A-D), desktop-dialog-i18n (E), desktop-platform-ui (F-G)
File follow-up for Intl.PluralRules polyfill — add structured warning + fallback to 'other' form
File follow-up for dialog-strings localization — needs cross-package locale bridge or bundled locale files in desktop package
